text.title: cost-reduction-strategies Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for cost-reduction-strategies as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Caching inside an agentic loop using session-scoped keys.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Agents in a loop must observe state changes between turns; session-scoped cache keys return stale tool-call results and cause the agent to act on outdated information.

failure-mode.description: Aggressive compression that strips semantic information from instructions (e.g., removing examples or detail from JSON schemas).
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Losing constraint details produces outputs that do not match the required format; quality loss outweighs token savings.

failure-mode.description: Using an LLM to classify prompt complexity and select the routing model.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: The routing call itself costs money and adds 200-500ms latency; for tasks shorter than 500 tokens the routing overhead exceeds the savings.
